Forums Trades News Calendar Market Brokers 7:03am Search User/Email: Password: Create Account Login Forums Trades News Calendar Market Brokers Forums Trades News Calendar Market Brokers Login Create Account 7:03am Traders Online 29,895 Page Layout Forex Trader Search Log In / Create Account User/Email: Password: Not registered yet? Forgot your password? Traders Online 29,895 Page Layout Forex Trader Forex Broker Activity Live Forex Spreads Live Gold Spreads Broker Guide Updates Majors About EUR/USD GBP/USD USD/JPY USD/CHF USD/CAD AUD/USD NZD/USD Gold/USD News / Latest Stories Stream US launches more strikes on Iran after commercial ship struck in Strait of Hormuz From msn.com | 2 hr 3 min ago | 1 comment The U.S. military said it launched another round of strikes on Iran after the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps struck a container ship traveling through the Strait of Hormuz on Sunday. A series of attacks between the U.S. and Iran over the past several days led President Donald Trump to declare the end of a ceasefire meant to halt the fighting that the ... Iran said to begin strikes on US forces From breakingthenews.net | 3 hr ago | 3 comments The Iranian military launched missiles targeting various US bases across the Middle East, according to preliminary information. The reports come in the immediate aftermath of another serious escalation in the region. Muwafiq al-Salti Airbase in eastern Jordan has come under attack, though there is no official confirmation. Meanwhile, Iranian media mentioned ... Iran's Press TV: Explosions reported in Bushehr, Asaluyeh, southern Iran From @financialjuice | 6 hr ago | 26 comments Iran's Press TV: Explosions reported in Bushehr, Asaluyeh, southern Iran BREAKING: Strikes are reportedly hitting Asaluyeh and Bushehr right now. -U.S. airstrikes are reported against Asaluyeh in southern Iran, with additional strikes in Bushehr -Asaluyeh is the onshore hub of the South Pars gas field, the crown jewel of Iran's energy economy… pic.twitter.com/gDw1gTPgLj BREAKING: Unconfirmed reports claim HIMARS launches from BAHRAIN toward Iran -Circulating reports describe rocket artillery firing from Bahrain toward Iranian territory, completely unconfirmed -If real, it would mark the first known ground-launched strikes on Iran from a… pic.twitter.com/R8uOmQuJXK At 7:15 p.m. ET today, U.S. Central Command forces began launching the third round of strikes this week against Iran after Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps forces blatantly attacked M/V GFS Galaxy, a Cyprus-flagged container ship transiting the Strait of Hormuz. A civilian crew… Iran blocks Hormuz again, says US breached deal From breakingthenews.net | 7 hr ago | 41 comments Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) Navy announced that the Strait of Hormuz will be "temporarily closed until further notice," and no ship will be allowed to sail through the waterway, blaming the United States for violating the memorandum of understanding (MoU). The Strait will remain blocked until the US "ceases its interference in [the] region," the navy said in a statement. "Should the aggressor, exploiting this incident (which it itself caused), commit any further transgressions, we shall respond forcefully, and additional enemy bases in the region will be targeted," the IRGC Navy warned, adding that the blame for such an escalation would fall on the US, Israel, and "countries that have placed their territories" at their disposal. IRGC fired a missile at a commercial cargo ship that tried to pass through the strait of Hormuz, U.S. official says. Ship was hit and suffered damages More News / Hot Story Iran blocks Hormuz again, says US breached deal From breakingthenews.net | 7 hr ago | 41 comments Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) Navy announced that the Strait of Hormuz will be "temporarily closed until further notice," and no ship will be allowed to sail through the waterway, blaming the United States for violating the memorandum of understanding (MoU). The Strait will remain blocked until the US "ceases its interference in [the] region," the navy said in a statement. "Should the aggressor, exploiting this incident (which it itself caused), commit any further transgressions, we shall respond forcefully, and additional enemy bases in the region will be targeted," the IRGC Navy warned, adding that the blame for such an escalation would fall on the US, Israel, and "countries that have placed their territories" at their disposal. IRGC fired a missile at a commercial cargo ship that tried to pass through the strait of Hormuz, U.S. official says.
